title: Permission grant model
description: Formal shape of permission-grants.yaml — the project's declared permission intent (per COR-028). A list of grants, each attaching a subject (an agent, the `all` baseline, or the `operator`) to one or more privileges, optionally scoped, with an allow/deny effect. Privileges are referenced as `[privilege-catalog:<id>]` typed tokens (COR-019). Document-shaped schema: the file IS the resource. Companion per COR-018 / the .pkit/schemas/ area conventions.

grants.description: The declared grants. Empty is valid (no intent declared).

subject.description: Who the grant is about: `all` (baseline applying to every agent), `operator` (the human driving the session), or `agent:<name>`.

privilege.description: One privilege, or a list of privileges, this grant covers — each a `[privilege-catalog:<id>]` token (COR-019).

scope.description: Where/when the grant applies (e.g. directory globs for a directory-scoped privilege). Absent = anywhere. A scoped allow denies the privilege outside the scope.

effect.description: Whether the grant allows or denies. Absent defaults to `allow`. Deny wins over allow across a subject's effective grant set.
